project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Docker Image Build & Local Run
sprints.goal: Fix .dockerignore bugs and build/run the gateway as a Docker container locally

features.title: Fix .dockerignore to allow Docker build to succeed
features.description: .dockerignore has two recursive exclusion patterns that break the Docker build: (1) `pnpm-workspace.yaml` is excluded but Dockerfile COPY commands need it for pnpm install, (2) `tsconfig.json` is excluded and the recursive match removes `apps/gateway/tsconfig.json` and `apps/admin/tsconfig.json` which tsc and vite need during build.
acceptance_criteria: Given .dockerignore is updated, when `docker compose build` runs, then pnpm install succeeds (pnpm-workspace.yaml is available in build context)
Given .dockerignore is updated, when `docker compose build` runs, then tsc can find tsconfig.json in apps/gateway/ and apps/admin/
Given the fix, when `docker compose build` runs, then the full multi-stage build completes without error
features.technical_notes: Remove `pnpm-workspace.yaml` line entirely. Change `tsconfig.json` to `/tsconfig.json` so it only excludes a root-level file (which doesn't exist anyway). The `vitest.config.ts` exclusion is harmless but also recursive — change to `/vitest.config.ts` for safety.

features.title: Build Docker image and verify gateway starts
features.description: Run `docker compose build` to build the multi-stage Docker image, then start the container with `docker compose up -d` and verify the gateway is healthy.
acceptance_criteria: Given .dockerignore is fixed, when running `docker compose build`, then image builds successfully
Given the image is built, when running `docker compose up -d`, then the container starts and health check passes
Given the container is running, when hitting `http://localhost:3000/health`, then response is `{"status":"ok",...}`
Given the container is running, when hitting `http://localhost:3000/admin`, then the admin SPA loads
features.technical_notes: docker-compose.yml mounts `apps/gateway/config.toml` to `/app/config.toml:ro`. The gateway loads config from CWD which is `/app`, so config.toml in the mount is at the correct path. The admin SPA is built during the Docker build and served by the gateway via serveAdminPlugin at `/admin/`.
